title: One-Way Data Flow
body:
## Definition

**One-way data flow** means data passes from parent to children via props; children notify parents via callback props — not by mutating parent data directly.

## Why it exists

Predictable debugging and clear ownership of who can change what.

## How it works

```
Parent state → props → Child
Child event → callback → Parent setState
```

Global stores (Redux, Zustand) still follow dispatch → new state → render.

## Common confusion

Context flows down too — still one direction for updates via Provider value changes.

## In practice

Contrast with two-way binding in some frameworks; foundation for React mental model.
bodyLinked:
## Definition

**[One-way data flow](concept:one-way-data-flow)** means data passes from parent to children via props; children notify parents via callback props — not by mutating parent data directly.

## Why it exists

Predictable debugging and clear ownership of who can change what.

## How it works

```
Parent state → props → Child
Child event → callback → Parent setState
```

Global stores (Redux, [Zustand](concept:zustand)) still follow dispatch → [new](concept:new-operator) state → render.

## Common confusion

[Context](concept:use-context) flows down too — still one direction for updates via Provider value changes.

## In practice

Contrast with two-way [binding](concept:binding) in [some](concept:array-some-every) frameworks; foundation for React mental model.
